rwanda_tech_companies.name: Andela
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: Andela places African engineers with international clients. Rigorous technical screening. Remote.

rwanda_tech_companies.name: AC Group
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: Behind the Tap&Go card used on Kigali buses. Product company with real users.

rwanda_tech_companies.name: Irembo
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: Public-private partnership. Irembo is how Rwandans access government services online.

rwanda_tech_companies.name: Bank of Kigali
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: Largest bank in Rwanda. Strong digital team. Stable employer, often hires junior talent.

rwanda_tech_companies.name: MTN Rwanda
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: MoMo is one of the largest mobile money platforms in Africa.

rwanda_tech_companies.name: Teta Tech
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: Local agency. Great first job for freelancers moving to full-time.

rwanda_tech_companies.name: RwandaOnline
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: Builds and maintains government digital infrastructure.

rwanda_tech_companies.name: Zipline
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: World-famous Rwanda-based drone company. Highly competitive to join.

rwanda_tech_companies.name: Awesomity Lab
rwanda_tech_companies.notes: One of Kigali's top design-focused agencies.

coding_analogies.variable: A variable is like a labelled envelope at the post office. The label is the name, the contents are the value. You can swap what is inside, but the label stays the same.
coding_analogies.function: A function is like an umuganda team — you tell them 'clean this street', they do their job, and they give you back a clean street. You do not care HOW they clean, you just care about the result.
coding_analogies.array: An array is like a line of students at assembly. Each student has a position (index), starting from 0. You can count them, line them up, or call the 3rd one forward.
coding_analogies.object: An object is like a national ID card. It has fields: name, date of birth, nationality, ID number. Each field has a value. You look up information by field name.
coding_analogies.loop: A loop is like harvesting a field of maize. You move from one plant to the next, doing the same action — pick the maize — until the field is empty. That is exactly what a for-loop does.
coding_analogies.conditional: An if-statement is like the checkpoint at the airport. IF your passport is valid, you pass through. ELSE you are turned away. The computer makes the same decision thousands of times per second.
coding_analogies.class: A class is like the blueprint for a house. The blueprint is not a house. But you can build many houses from the same blueprint. Each house (object) has the same rooms but can have different paint colours.
coding_analogies.api: An API is like the waiter at a restaurant. You (the client) do not go into the kitchen (the server). You tell the waiter what you want (request), they bring it back to you (response). The waiter is the API.
coding_analogies.database: A database is like the Rwanda national ID registry. It stores millions of records, each one structured the same way, and you can search by ID number to find any citizen instantly.
coding_analogies.git: Git is like version control for your group assignment. Every time someone makes a change, Git takes a snapshot. If someone ruins the document, you can go back to any previous snapshot. No more 'final_v2_REAL_final.docx'.
coding_analogies.cache: A cache is like keeping sugar on your kitchen counter instead of the cupboard. You use it often, so you keep it close. Caches make programs fast by keeping frequently-used data nearby.
coding_analogies.recursion: Recursion is like nesting baskets. You open a basket, inside is another basket, inside is another, until you find the smallest one with the answer. Then you close them back up, one by one.

job_search_resources.name: JobInRwanda
job_search_resources.description: The main job board for Rwanda. Most Kigali tech jobs are posted here first.

job_search_resources.name: BrighterMonday Rwanda
job_search_resources.description: Regional African job board with strong Rwanda listings.

job_search_resources.name: LinkedIn Jobs
job_search_resources.description: International and local jobs. Filter by 'Rwanda' or 'Remote'.

job_search_resources.name: Upwork
job_search_resources.description: The largest freelance marketplace. Remote work that pays in USD.

job_search_resources.name: Fiverr
job_search_resources.description: Gig-based freelance site. Good for quick wins and building reviews.

job_search_resources.name: Andela
job_search_resources.description: Talent network placing African engineers with international companies.

job_search_resources.name: Turing
job_search_resources.description: Remote software engineering jobs with US-based companies. Rigorous screening.

job_search_resources.name: Toptal
job_search_resources.description: Elite freelance network. Top 3% of applicants. Pays very well.

job_search_resources.name: Remote OK
job_search_resources.description: Open remote job board. Global positions, many of which are Africa-friendly.

job_search_resources.name: We Work Remotely
job_search_resources.description: One of the oldest and biggest remote job boards.

junior_frontend_developer.notes: Entry-level React or Vue developer in Kigali.

full_stack_developer.notes: Can work across frontend and backend.

digital_marketer.notes: Social media, paid ads, content strategy.

seo_specialist.notes: Technical SEO + content SEO + link building.

remote_junior_developer_usd.notes: Remote role for international company. Requires strong English and portfolio.

remote_senior_developer_usd.notes: Senior engineer with 4+ years for a US or EU startup via Andela/Turing.

interview_culture_tips: Kigali tech interviews are more formal than Silicon Valley. Dress business casual even for virtual interviews.
Always greet the interviewer in their preferred language first (Kinyarwanda, English, or French) — shows respect.
Do not interrupt. Africans value letting elders and authority figures finish speaking.
Be humble about achievements. Brag indirectly through stories, not directly.
Mention community work or Umuganda — Rwandan employers value civic contribution.
Never badmouth previous employers or teachers. Ever.
When asked 'What is your weakness?', have a real answer. Not 'I work too hard'.
Follow up within 24 hours with a short thank-you email mentioning one specific thing from the conversation.
